docs.text:

"When things are a disappointment, try not to be so discouraged." — Carol Burnett Source

docs.title: Quick Summary
docs.text: Date Milestone Notes 02‑07‑2025 ✅ Explored MkDocs commands Ranmkdocs servefor the first time 03‑07‑2025 ✅ Learned basic commands build / deploy / serve Understood what each command does and its output 04‑07‑2025 ✅ Created a simple site navigation Added multiple pages and configured nav in mkdocs.yml 05‑07‑2025 ✅ Improved site structure and formatting Renamed TOC to “My Learning Checklist”, fixed layout & headings

docs.title: My Learning Checklist
docs.text:
• [x] Learn Markdown basics
  • [x] Deploy with GitHub Pages
  • [x] Workflow with GitHub Actions
  • [x] Customize theme and layout

docs.title: Why I'm doing this
docs.text: I want to build solid documentation skills and share what I learn along the way. This page will grow as I continue exploring Markdown and documentation best practices.

docs.title: 📜 Changelog
docs.text:
• 05‑07‑2025: Improved formatting and content
  • 03‑07‑2025: First draft completed

docs.title: Markdown Basics Part 02
docs.text: 📅 Created: 05‑07‑2025 🛠️ Last updated: 05‑07‑2025 🔖 Version: v1.0

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: In Basics Part 01, we have walked through the basic Markdown elements, now let's take a look at other Markdown elements such as Lists (Unorderd and Ordered Lists), Link, Blockquote and Code block.

docs.title: Basic formatting
docs.text:
Below is a quick reference table so you can immediately see the Markdown syntax (code snippet) side‑by‑side with the rendered result.

Purpose Markdown syntax (code) Rendered Unordered List - Item - Item Ordered List 1. Item 1. Item Link [OpenAI](...) OpenAI

docs.title: Markdown
docs.text: > Quoted text

docs.title: Rendered output
docs.text: Quoted text

docs.title: Code block
docs.text:
````
python
def hello():
    print("Hello, Markdown!")
````

docs.title: Rendered output
docs.text:
def hello():
    print("Hello, Markdown!")

docs.title: 📜 Changelog
docs.text: • 05‑07‑2025: First draft completed

docs.title: Markdown Basics
docs.text: 📅 Created: 03‑07‑2025 🛠️ Last updated: 05‑07‑2025 🔖 Version: v1.0

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Markdown is a lightweight markup language to create formatted documents using plain text.

docs.title: Basic text formatting
docs.text:
Below is a quick reference table so you can immediately see the Markdown syntax (code snippet) side‑by‑side with the rendered result.

Purpose Markdown syntax (code) Rendered Heading level 1 # Heading 1 # Heading 1 Heading level 2 ## Heading 2 ## Heading 2 Heading level 3 ### Heading 3 ### Heading 3 Italic *italic* italic Bold **bold** bold Bold + Italic ***bold italic*** bold italic Strikethrough ~~strikethrough~~ ~~strikethrough~~
